articles.summary: It shouldn't take 5 clicks through dashboards to figure out how many visitors were on your website yesterday. Ahrefs Web Analytics [4] is the simple and free Google Analytics alternative [4]:

articles.title: FREE for up to 1M events/month. Get started now
HOW AI-GENERATED CONTENT PERFORMS IN GOOGLE SEARCH: A 16-MONTH EXPERIMENT
articles.summary: AI content can get indexed and gain early traffic fast, but it'll fail to sustain the rankings without SEO fundamentals. In this experiment, 71% of pages were indexed in 36 days and generated 122,000+ impressions, then grew to 526,000+ impressions by month 3, but only 3% of pages remained in the top 100 after that point. Around 70% of total traffic came in the first 2.5 months, then growth slowed, and visibility dropped sharply due to weak authority, lack of unique insights, and missing trust signals. Adding new AI content later increased impressions by up to 17x to 19x for older pages, but gains were short-term and did not fix the long-term ranking issues.

articles.title: UX WORKS THROUGH SOCIAL RELATIONSHIPS. AI TOOLS ARE ERASING THEM
articles.summary: AI is eroding the social relationships that make design valuable, which leaves designers stuck cleaning up stakeholder-generated output instead of shaping strategy. Design has always balanced complexity and regularization, but AI pushes teams toward over-reliance on templates and patterns, which reduces original thinking and turns design into repetitive output. This shift replaces collective “meaning-making” with isolated production, which increases noise and slows work. The core problem is not tooling but the breakdown of relationships, since real value comes from sharing ideas, getting feedback, and discovering what is wrong early. Improving AI skills does not fix this issue because it strengthens a system that avoids listening, which leads to generic solutions and weaker outcomes.

articles.title: THE EXACT TITLE TAG FORMULAS BEHIND TOP-RANKING PAGES
articles.summary: Title tags now impact visibility across search, AI tools, and link previews, not just Google. Unclear or overly creative titles reduce clicks and limit how content is surfaced by AI. Clear, intent-driven titles perform best. Focus on one keyword, stay under 60 characters, and match the page's H1.

articles.title: STACKED (VS SINGLE) DISCOUNTS WORK BETTER
articles.summary: Stacked discounts drive higher purchase intent and engagement than a single discount of the same total value. Splitting a 25% discount into multiple parts increased shopping intent by up to 15.8% and drove 66.3% more views, 52.7% more upvotes, and 52.3% more comments across 9,000+ deals. People see stacked offers as 33.1% more unique and feel 20.7% smarter when using them. The effect is stronger when the total discount is smaller and weaker when stacking feels complex or requires too much effort. Clear and simple stacking improves results and makes customers more likely to act.

articles.title: SURREAL: A MASTERCLASS IN UNHINGED MARKETING
articles.summary: Surreal broke into the saturated cereal market by positioning itself between sugary kids' cereals and bland health options, offering high-protein, low-sugar products wrapped in chaotic humor. The brand built a cult following on LinkedIn by targeting marketers as an amplification channel, knowing they'd share clever campaigns organically. Its paid media leans on fake celebrity billboards, absurd brand collaborations like "Dild-Os" with Lovehoney, and anti-marketing aesthetics that interrupt the patterns consumers expect from advertising.

articles.title: WHY SMALL BRANDS MUST USE TV (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This webinar, on March 25, will explain why TV is a powerful growth channel for smaller brands that have hit a plateau, showing how it can drive up to 35% more sales even with limited budgets. It covers why social media alone often fails to build awareness, how big brands benefit from scale in ways smaller brands cannot replicate, and how adding TV can unlock reach and credibility. It also explores how to combine TV with channels like CTV, podcasts, and OOH.

articles.title: I BUILT 5 MARKETING TOOLS WITH AHREFS FIREHOSE API PART 1
articles.summary: This guide shows how to use Ahrefs Firehose API to build tools that monitor the web in real time and trigger actions. Using lightweight scripts and daily scheduled jobs, you can track brand mentions, competitors, and keywords with precise filters like page type or domain. One example sends a daily Slack digest of mentions using custom rules. The system follows a clear pattern where Firehose captures signals, scripts process them, and results are delivered where teams work.

articles.title: YOUR WRITER DOESN'T WANT TO BE YOUR CONTENT STRATEGIST
articles.summary: Content strategy often fails when companies ask writers to own it. Writing and strategy require different skills. Writers focus on execution, while strategy defines audience and ties content to revenue. Confusing job titles lead to underqualified hires. Teams often expect one person to do everything, which creates output without direction. Companies should separate roles. Hire a strategist to build the foundation, then bring in marketers or managers to execute.

articles.title: CHATGPT ADS PILOT LEAVES ADVERTISERS WITHOUT PROOF OF ROI
articles.summary: Early advertisers testing ChatGPT ads say the product isn't ready for performance marketing. There is no programmatic buying, minimal targeting, and almost no data to evaluate outcomes. Two agency executives confirmed they couldn't demonstrate measurable business results from their spend. OpenAI plans to roll out ads to all free and low-cost US users soon, but without proper measurement tools, the channel remains a blind bet for now.

articles.title: SEO TIP: IF YOU WANT TO UNDERSTAND HOW SEARCH REALLY WORKS, READ ENGINEERING WRITE-UPS
articles.summary: The way retrieval systems work explains why phrase structure, passage structure, internal context, and semantic proximity matter so much for content visibility.

articles.title: THE REVERSE JOB SEARCH METHOD
articles.summary: Steps in searching for the people behind job posts on LinkedIn.

articles.title: INSTAGRAM NOW LETS YOU REARRANGE PHOTOS IN A CAROUSEL AFTER POSTING
articles.summary: The feature works by tapping Edit on a post, then holding and dragging images into a new order.
